steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence: "When you place your ______ the pitch will shift as your hands move through the air." The blank fills "hands between the two antennas," followed by a comma. Both words are plain plurals — more than one hand, more than one antenna. Neither word shows possession, so neither takes an apostrophe. The comma after the blank is the needed separator before the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — hand's between the two antenna's,: "...your hand's between the two antenna's, the pitch will shift..." Both apostrophes signal possession; nothing is owned by hands or antennas here. ✗
  • Choice B — hands between the two antennas,: "...your hands between the two antennas, the pitch will shift..." Plain plurals, no apostrophes, comma correctly separating the introductory phrase from the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — hands' between the two antennas',: "...your hands' between the two antennas', the pitch will shift..." Both apostrophes wrongly show possession where none exists; extra comma after "antennas'" is also incorrect. ✗
  • Choice D — hands' between the two antennas,: "...your hands' between the two antennas, the pitch will shift..." "hands'" incorrectly marks possession; only the plural is needed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
